name: encrypt_package
short_description: Protect a packaged IP archive with an encryption wrapper.
full_help:
encrypt_package

Description: 
Protect a packaged IP archive with an encryption wrapper.

Syntax: 
encrypt_package  [-customer_key <arg>] -ip_partner_private_key <arg>
                 [-ip_partner_keyowner <arg>] -ip_partner_envelope <arg>
                 [-output <arg>] [-exclude <arg>] [-quiet] [-verbose]
                 <input>...

Usage: 
  Name                     Description
  ------------------------------------
  [-customer_key]          The name of a PEM format file containing the IP 
                           customer's RSA public key. If not specified a key 
                           pair will be generated and the private key 
                           installed on the user's host when 
                           install_encrypted_package is run.
  -ip_partner_private_key  The name of a PEM format file containing the IP 
                           partner's RSA private key. If the name specified 
                           does not include a file extension, .pem will be 
                           assumed.
  [-ip_partner_keyowner]   If specified, will replace the value of 
                           key_keyowner in the encryption envelope specified 
                           by -ip_partner_envelope.
  -ip_partner_envelope     Filename of the file containing the encryption 
                           envelope - the IP partner's public RSA key and the
                           rights being granted the user. Note that this file
                           is not included in the zip file in any form, it is
                           only used to encrypt the HDL files in the package.
  [-output]                Name of the output archive file. If not specified 
                           and the input is an archive, the input archive 
                           file will be overwritten. If the input is a 
                           directory, this name of the output archive must be
                           specified.
  [-exclude]               If input is directory, this option can be used to 
                           specify a comma-separated list of files to exclude
                           from the packaged archive. Not valid if input is 
                           an archive.
  [-quiet]                 Ignore command errors
  [-verbose]               Suspend message limits during command execution
  <input>                  Either the name of the input archive (zip) file, 
                           which should have been generated using the Vivado 
                           IP Packager to package an IP, or the name of a 
                           directory containing the files to include. If a 
                           directory is specified, it will be searched 
                           recursively for all the files to include in the 
                           output archive. HDL files will be encrypted.

Categories: 
FileIO

arguments.description: The name of a PEM format file containing the IP customer's RSA public key. If not specified a key pair will be generated and the private key installed on the user's host when install_encrypted_package is run.
The name of a PEM format file containing the IP partner's RSA private key. If the name specified does not include a file extension, .pem will be assumed.
If specified, will replace the value of key_keyowner in the encryption envelope specified by -ip_partner_envelope.

Filename of the file containing the encryption envelope - the IP partner's public RSA key and the rights being granted the user. Note that this file is not included in the zip file in any form, it is only used to encrypt the HDL files in the package.

Name of the output archive file. If not specified and the input is an archive, the input archive file will be overwritten. If the input is a directory, this name of the output archive must be specified.
If input is directory, this option can be used to specify a comma-separated list of files to exclude from the packaged archive. Not valid if input is an archive.
Suspend message limits during command execution
